content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN such that:


2√ó(A1‚äïA2‚äï‚Ä¶‚äïAN)‚â•(A1‚à£A2‚à£‚Ä¶‚à£AN)2\times (A_1 \oplus A_2 \oplus \ldots \oplus A_N) \geq (A_1 | A_2 | \ldots | A_N)2√ó(A1‚Äã‚äïA2‚Äã‚äï‚Ä¶‚äïAN‚Äã)‚â•(A1‚Äã‚à£A2‚Äã‚à£‚Ä¶‚à£AN‚Äã), where ‚äï\oplus‚äï and ‚à£|‚à£ denote the bitwise xor and or operations respectively.
Your task is to perform the following operation until the array is empty:


Choose any element AiA_iAi‚Äã from the array.
Remove AiA_iAi‚Äã from the array.
Perform **one** of the following:

Use a new box and add AiA_iAi‚Äã to the box.
Select an existing box such that the XOR of all the elements in the box (before placing AiA_iAi‚Äã) is **greater than or equal to** AiA_iAi‚Äã and add AiA_iAi‚Äã to the box.


Determine the **minimum** number of boxes required to empty the array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN ‚Äî the number of elements in AAA.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of boxes required to empty the array AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

Choose 666 from the array and delete it.

Add 666 to a new box.



AAA is empty now and we used one box.
Test case 222:

Choose 121212 from the array and delete it.

Add 121212 to a new box. The XOR of the box becomes 121212.


Choose 333 from the array and delete it.

Since the XOR of the box 12‚â•312 \geq 312‚â•3, we can add 333 to the box. The XOR of the box becomes 12‚äï3=1512\oplus3=1512‚äï3=15.


Choose 222 from the array and delete it.

Since the XOR of the box 15‚â•215 \geq 215‚â•2, we can add 222 to the box. The XOR of the box becomes 15‚äï2=1315\oplus2=1315‚äï2=13.


Choose 111 from the array and delete it.

Since the XOR of the box 13‚â•113 \geq 113‚â•1, we can add 111 to the box. The XOR of the box becomes 13‚äï1=1213\oplus1=1213‚äï1=12.



AAA is empty now and we used one box.
Test case 333:

Choose 555 from the array and delete it.

Add 555 to a new box. The XOR of the box becomes 555.


Choose 444 from the array and delete it.

Since the XOR of the box 5‚â•45 \geq 45‚â•4, we can add 444 to the box. The XOR of the box becomes 5‚äï4=15\oplus4=15‚äï4=1.


Choose 666 from the array and delete it.

Add 666 to a new box. The XOR of the second box is 666.



AAA is now empty and we used two boxes.
